title: stenotaphrum dimidiatum var. secundatum
intro: Stenotaphrum dimidiatum var. secundatum, also known as St. Augustine grass, is a type of warm-season grass native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of St. Augustine grass include its ability to reduce stress, improve sleep, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
